channel.title: Prompting on Chen Kai Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Prompting on Chen Kai Blog

item.title: LLM Engineering (9): Prompting at Production Scale
item.description:
A prompt that works on 100 examples in a notebook can fail on 10 % of inputs in production for reasons that have nothing to do with cleverness. This chapter is about prompting as engineering: where chain-of-thought actually helps (and where it doesn’t), how prompt caching changes the cost equation, and how to defend against the jailbreaks and injections that production traffic will produce within a week of launch.

Chain-of-thought: useful, but not always

“Let’s think step by step” — the original CoT trick (Wei et al., 2022) — added reasoning chains to LLM outputs and improved math, logic, and multi-step QA performance dramatically. By 2024 every chat model defaulted to some form of reasoning when prompted.
